title: Immigration Analytics, Benchmarking and Knowledge | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
## Immigration Analytics, Benchmarking and Knowledge
Understand immigration in a changing world.
The immigration environment worldwide is uncertain and, in many respects, in a state of flux.
Government enforcement is increasing, and regulations and policies are changing at lightning speed. At times, it can seem like a full-time job just to keep up—especially as the world continues to adjust to our complicated pandemic landscape.
Fragomen has dedicated a group of professionals around the world who are solely devoted to monitoring and advising on legislative and regulatory changes in immigration. We harness this intelligence to provide a range of knowledge services to help you understand immigration in our changing world.

### The latest immigration knowledge at your fingertips.
We empower our clients by providing them with current information and insights.
We’ve developed a range of dynamic immigration tools and resources, updated daily to help support your decision making, including:
- Worldwide Immigration Trends reports Over 600 immigration alerts and updates a year Insights and perspectives Our Knowledge Center, which is available to clients via our Connect case management portal
- Worldwide Immigration Trends reports Over 600 immigration alerts and updates a year Insights and perspectives Our Knowledge Center, which is available to clients via our Connect case management portal
- Worldwide Immigration Trends reports
- Over 600 immigration alerts and updates a year
- Insights and perspectives
- Our Knowledge Center, which is available to clients via our Connect case management portal

### Deep dive immigration knowledge databases.
We monitor information daily and maintain comprehensive databases to produce a wealth of knowledge products for our clients, which include detailed information on immigration processes, immigration process videos, red flag issues, heat maps, processing times, and other information relevant to immigration compliance and your immigration program management.

### Knowledge consulting services.
We also offer robust advisory support, including:
- Multi-jurisdictional information and analysis on specific topics Guidance and project management for corporate restructurings and other special projects Immigration policy assistance Legislative, regulatory and policy analysis
- Multi-jurisdictional information and analysis on specific topics Guidance and project management for corporate restructurings and other special projects Immigration policy assistance Legislative, regulatory and policy analysis
- Multi-jurisdictional information and analysis on specific topics
- Guidance and project management for corporate restructurings and other special projects
- Immigration policy assistance
- Legislative, regulatory and policy analysis
These products and capabilities augment our advisory and compliance services to help educate our clients and inform their immigration programs.

### The program data you need, when you need it.
Immigration is but one of many corporate functions with expectations to live up to and budgets to meet. As an immigration stakeholder, you need access to your program data and analytics as quickly as possible.
We utilize suites of analytics tools including:
- A wide range of visualized program reporting analytics from our Connect case management portal Consulting tools to complement strategic advice such as predictive analysis and risk mitigation Internal workflow optimization and data integrity tools
- A wide range of visualized program reporting analytics from our Connect case management portal Consulting tools to complement strategic advice such as predictive analysis and risk mitigation Internal workflow optimization and data integrity tools
- A wide range of visualized program reporting analytics from our Connect case management portal
- Consulting tools to complement strategic advice such as predictive analysis and risk mitigation
- Internal workflow optimization and data integrity tools
Our analytics portfolio drives speed, accuracy and efficiency to new levels.

### How does your immigration program stack up?
Savvy companies use immigration strategically to attract the talent they need in an increasingly competitive marketplace. To do so, businesses must understand how their immigration benefits compare to those of their competitors.
Fragomen processes hundreds of thousands of immigration cases a year. This vast amount of empirical data allows us to:
- Track, compare and report on a wide variety of factors associated with case processing and program management Formulate unique insights and predict trends Benchmark best practices based on the wide range of practices and procedures our clients utilize and those relevant to specific industries Develop meaningful metrics that allow you to effectively monitor your immigration programs.
- Track, compare and report on a wide variety of factors associated with case processing and program management Formulate unique insights and predict trends Benchmark best practices based on the wide range of practices and procedures our clients utilize and those relevant to specific industries Develop meaningful metrics that allow you to effectively monitor your immigration programs.
- Track, compare and report on a wide variety of factors associated with case processing and program management
- Formulate unique insights and predict trends
- Benchmark best practices based on the wide range of practices and procedures our clients utilize and those relevant to specific industries
- Develop meaningful metrics that allow you to effectively monitor your immigration programs.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
